question: Compare the NuScale RPV materials with those used in conventional PWR vessels.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel over its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits throughout the vessel’s operational lifespan.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, with the RPV forming the SG shell and pressure boundary, and the SG tubes and welds contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, providing a critical barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component, supporting various systems and providing a barrier to fission product release, with materials and fabrication standards selected to ensure integrity throughout the plant’s design life.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, heater sheaths, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure, and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, while providing continuous monitoring and control through instrumentation.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) complies with relevant regulatory requirements, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria, by designing, fabricating, and testing reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Class 1 standards. It also clarifies the classification of connected piping and references the applicable ASME BPVC edition and regulatory guides used for the design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, inspection, and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, and surveillance requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards for quality assurance and material integrity. It specifies procedures for maintaining a dry, controlled environment during shipment and details where further information on inspections, operating conditions, and fasteners can be found. Key references to industry codes and standards are also provided.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and comply with relevant U.S. regulatory requirements to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance. These materials are subject to rigorous testing and quality assurance measures, with detailed specifications provided in referenced tables and sections.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the use of ASME and NRC methodologies to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides figures and tables with the applicable limits to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

edges.description: Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity by maintaining pressure-temperature limits, while Section B describes the components contained within the NuScale RPV, including the steam generators, both providing context for the operational and design environment of the RPV materials.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits for the NuScale RPV are not exceeded, which is directly dependent on the material properties and design described in Section B.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure the NuScale RPV operates within pressure-temperature limits, while Section B describes the physical configuration and function of the NuScale RPV, with both sections connected by their focus on maintaining the integrity and safe operation of the reactor pressure vessel.

Section A describes the overall components housed within the NuScale RPV, including steam generators, while Section B focuses specifically on the materials and structural role of the RPV itself, with both sections connecting through their discussion of the RPV's design and function in the reactor coolant system.

Section A provides a general overview of the components contained within the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, including steam generators, while Section B specifically details the reactor vessel itself and its role as a pressure-retaining component, with both sections connecting through their descriptions of the RPV's contents and function.

Section A specifies the materials and components of the NuScale RPV and references other sections for material details, while Section B discusses the operational procedures to ensure vessel integrity, both being connected by the need to ensure that the selected RPV materials can withstand operational transients and comply with technical specifications.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to other sections (including Section 5.2 and 4.5) for material descriptions relevant to the RPV components, which are part of the overall RPV system described in Section B.
Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for details on the RPV upper shell and upper head materials, which are the focus of Section B.

Section A describes the design and function of the pressurizer as an integral part of the NuScale reactor vessel, while Section B addresses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity by maintaining pressure-temperature limits, connecting them through their mutual concern with pressure management in the reactor vessel.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel, while Section B lists the pressurizer (along with steam generators and other components) as part of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, connecting them by specifying the pressurizer's inclusion in the overall RPV design.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the NuScale reactor vessel, while Section B provides an overview of the reactor vessel and its components, establishing that the pressurizer is included within the RPV, thus connecting the design integration of the pressurizer with the overall vessel structure.

Section A defines the scope and regulatory basis for the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B describes the operational procedures developed to ensure that the reactor vessel (a key RCPB component) operates within specified pressure-temperature limits, thus maintaining the integrity described in Section A.
Section B specifies the components included within the NuScale RPV, providing details that clarify the scope of the RCPB described in Section A.

Section B provides specific details about the materials and components of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity is discussed in general terms in Section A.

Section A establishes that the NuScale RPV complies with ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits (also derived from ASME BPVC) are not exceeded, thus both sections are connected through their reliance on the same code requirements for vessel integrity.

Section A states that the NuScale RPV complies with ASME BPVC Section III requirements, which are the same standards typically applied to conventional PWR vessels as described in Section B's context of RPV design.

Section A states that the NuScale RPV materials comply with ASME BPVC Section III and regulatory requirements, while Section B describes the physical components and context of the NuScale RPV, thus Section A specifies the regulatory compliance details for the general vessel described in Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that inform the selection and qualification of RPV materials, while Section B discusses the operational procedures developed to ensure the integrity of the reactor vessel under specified conditions, both of which are connected through their reliance on material properties and regulatory compliance for reactor vessel safety.

Section A provides references to material standards and guidelines that are relevant for evaluating the integrity and material selection of the NuScale RPV, while Section B describes the components contained within the NuScale RPV, establishing the context in which those material standards are applied.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that establish material requirements and quality assurance for reactor pressure vessels, while Section B describes the specific materials and configuration of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, thus connecting the general regulatory framework to the specific design implementation.
Section A references Section B by specifying that the materials and components of the pressurizer, which is described in Section B, are detailed in Section A and related subsections.
Section A specifies the materials used in the NuScale RPV and its components, which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity requirements are described in Section B.

Section A describes the specific materials used in the NuScale RPV and its components, while Section B states that these materials and components comply with the ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a regulatory requirements, thus connecting the design details to regulatory compliance.

Section A describes the specific materials used in the NuScale RPV and references Section 5.2 for further details, while Section B lists industry standards and guidelines (such as EPRI and ASME) that define acceptable materials and quality assurance for PWR vessels, thus connecting the actual material choices in NuScale to the conventional regulatory and industry context.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel, which is one of the pressure-retaining components included in the reactor coolant pressure boundary discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the design and function of the NuScale reactor vessel's pressurizer, while Section B states that the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and associated components, including the pressurizer, comply with ASME BPVC requirements, thus connecting the specific design (A) to the regulatory compliance (B).

Section A describes the design and function of the NuScale pressurizer as part of the reactor vessel, while Section B lists references that include industry guidelines and standards relevant to reactor coolant boundary materials, connecting them through the regulatory and technical context for material selection and qualification.

Section A describes the design features ensuring the integrity of the NuScale reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies that these components comply with ASME BPVC Section III requirements, thus providing the regulatory standards that the materials and design in Section A must meet.

Section A describes the design features and regulatory definitions ensuring the integrity of the NuScale reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references—including industry guidelines and materials reliability programs—that provide the technical basis and standards for material selection and evaluation in Section A.

Section A asserts compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements for RPV materials, while Section B lists references (including ASME and EPRI documents) that provide the technical standards and guidelines underpinning those compliance claims.

Section A specifies the ASME and NQA-1 standards for RPV material handling and storage, which are prerequisites for the operational procedures in Section B that ensure vessel integrity under pressure-temperature limits.

Section A details the inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the NuScale RPV materials, while Section B describes the overall design and components contained within the NuScale RPV, thus Section A provides specific procedural requirements for the materials referenced in the broader system context of Section B.

Section A references the inspection and handling requirements for the NuScale RPV materials, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV and its role in the reactor system, connecting them through the subject of RPV materials and their regulatory context.

Section A specifies the material standards and regulatory compliance for the NuScale RPV, while Section B references the need for operating procedures to ensure that the vessel's pressure-temperature limits, which depend on those material properties, are not exceeded.

Section A specifies the material standards and regulatory compliance for the NuScale RPV, while Section B identifies the RPV as the component housing the reactor and steam generators, thus connecting the materials discussed in A to the vessel context in B.

Section A specifies that the NuScale RPV materials conform to ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV and its role in the reactor system, with Section A referencing Section B for detailed material conformance.

Section A describes the handling and storage requirements for the NuScale RPV in accordance with ASME BPVC and NQA-1 standards, while Section B specifies that the RPV materials conform to ASME BPVC Section II and III requirements, directly connecting the material selection and handling processes to regulatory and code compliance.

Section A provides technical details and code compliance for RPV material welds and attachments, while Section B discusses the operational procedures developed to ensure that the pressure-temperature limits—derived from the material properties and analyses in Section A—are not exceeded, directly connecting material characteristics to operational safety requirements.

Section A discusses the examination and code compliance of RPV materials and welds, while Section B identifies the RPV as housing key components including steam generators, connecting them by specifying the RPV's role and the standards its materials must meet.

Section A discusses the examination and welding requirements for RPV attachments per ASME BPVC, while Section B provides a general description of the NuScale RPV and its role in the reactor system, with both sections referencing the construction and regulatory standards for RPV materials.
